title: The Legend Begins (Samkshepa Ramayanam)
source_owner: Ralph T. H. Griffith translation via Project Gutenberg; Sanskrit digital source pending verification
revision_note: Explicit-only source-audit pending starter. Built from src/lib/data/ramayana-bal-kanda-1.ts; meanings are Shoonaya literal study renderings cross-checked against Griffith, not Griffith verbatim.

content.sanskrit:
तपःस्वाध्यायनिरतं तपस्वी वाग्विदां वरम् ।
नारदं परिपप्रच्छ वाल्मीकिर्मुनिपुंगवम् ॥
content.transliteration:
tapaḥ-svādhyāya-nirataṃ tapasvī vāgvidāṃ varam |
nāradaṃ paripapraccha vālmīkir-munipuṃgavam ||
content.translation: Ascetic Valmiki enquired of Narada, the preeminent among the sages, who was ever engaged in austerities and self-study of the Vedas, and who was the foremost among the eloquent.
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
को न्वस्मिन्साम्प्रतं लोके गुणवान्कश्च वीर्यवान् ।
धर्मज्ञश्च कृतज्ञश्च सत्यवाक्यो दृढव्रतः ॥
content.transliteration:
ko nv-asmins-sāmprataṃ loke guṇavān-kaśca vīryavān |
dharmajñaś-ca kṛtajñaś-ca satyavākyo dṛḍhavrataḥ ||
content.translation: Who in this present world is endowed with all virtues? Who is mighty? Who knows righteousness? Who is grateful? Who is truthful? Who is firm in his vows?
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
चारित्रेण च को युक्तः सर्वभूतेषु को हितः ।
विद्वान्कः कः समर्थश्च कश्चैकप्रियदर्शनः ॥
content.transliteration:
cāritreṇa ca ko yuktaḥ sarva-bhūteṣu ko hitaḥ |
vidvān-kaḥ kaḥ samarthaś-ca kaś-caika-priyadarśanaḥ ||
content.translation: Who is endowed with good character? Who is the benefactor of all living beings? Who is the most learned? Who is the most capable? Who is uniquely pleasing to the sight?
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
आत्मवान्को जितक्रोधो द्युतिमान्कोऽनसूयकः ।
कस्य बिभ्यति देवाश्च जातरोषस्य संयुगे ॥
content.transliteration:
ātmavān-ko jitakrodho dyutimān-ko'nasūyakaḥ |
kasya bibhyati devāś-ca jāta-roṣasya saṃyuge ||
content.translation: Who is self-possessed? Who has conquered anger? Who is brilliant? Who is free from envy? Whose wrath, when provoked in battle, do even the gods fear?
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
एतदिच्छाम्यहं श्रोतुं परं कौतूहलं हि मे ।
महर्षे त्वं समर्थोऽसि ज्ञातुमेवंविधं नरम् ॥
content.transliteration:
etad-icchāmy-ahaṃ śrotuṃ paraṃ kautūhalaṃ hi me |
maharṣe tvaṃ samartho'si jñātum-evaṃvidhaṃ naram ||
content.translation: I desire to hear this, for I have great curiosity. O great sage (Narada), you are capable of knowing such a man.
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
श्रुत्वा चैतत् त्रिलोकज्ञो वाल्मीकेर्नारदो वचः ।
श्रूयतामिति चामन्त्र्य प्रहृष्टो वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥
content.transliteration:
śrutvā caitat trilokajño vālmīkernārado vacaḥ |
śrūyatāmiti cāmantrya prahṛṣṭo vākyamabravīt ||
content.translation: Hearing these words of Valmiki, Sage Narada, who knows the three worlds, joyfully said 'Listen!', and spoke these words.
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
बहवो दुर्लभाश्चैव ये त्वया कीर्तिता गुणाः ।
मुने वक्ष्याम्यहं बुद्ध्वा तैर्युक्तः श्रूयतां नरः ॥
content.transliteration:
bahavo durlabhāścaiva ye tvayā kīrtitā guṇāḥ |
mune vakṣyāmyahaṃ buddhvā tairyuktaḥ śrūyatāṃ naraḥ ||
content.translation: O Sage! The many virtues that you have described are indeed rare. Having considered them, I shall tell you of a man endowed with them. Listen!
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
इक्ष्वाकुवंशप्रभवो रामो नाम जनैः श्रुतः ।
नियतात्मा महावीर्यो द्युतिमान् धृतिमान् वशी ॥
content.transliteration:
ikṣvākuvaṃśaprabhavo rāmo nāma janaiḥ śrutaḥ |
niyatātmā mahāvīryo dyutimān dhṛtimān vaśī ||
content.translation: There is one known among the people by the name of Rama, born in the lineage of Ikshvaku. He has a disciplined mind, is highly valorous, radiant, resolute, and in control of his senses.
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
बुद्धिमान् नीतिमान् वाग्मी श्रीमान् शत्रुनिबर्हणः ।
विपुलांसो महाबाहुः कम्बुग्रीवो महाहनुः ॥
content.transliteration:
buddhimān nītimān vāgmī śrīmān śatrunibarhaṇaḥ |
vipulāṃso mahābāhuḥ kambugrīvo mahāhanuḥ ||
content.translation: He is intelligent, well-versed in polity, eloquent, glorious, and the destroyer of enemies. He has broad shoulders, mighty arms, a neck like a conch shell, and a prominent jaw.
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
महोरस्को महेष्वासो गूढजत्रुररिन्दमः ।
आजानुबाहुः सुशिराः सुललाटः सुविक्रमः ॥
content.transliteration:
mahorasko maheṣvāso gūḍhajatrurarindamaḥ |
ājānubāhuḥ suśirāḥ sulalāṭaḥ suvikramaḥ ||
content.translation: He has a broad chest, a mighty bow, concealed collarbones, and is the subduer of foes. His arms reach down to his knees; he has a noble head, a broad forehead, and great prowess.
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
समः समविभक्ताङ्गः स्निग्धवर्णः प्रतापवान् ।
पीनवक्षा विशालाक्षो लक्ष्मीवान् शुभलक्षणः ॥
content.transliteration:
samaḥ samavibhaktāṅgaḥ snigdhavarṇaḥ pratāpavān |
pīnavakṣā viśālākṣo lakṣmīvān śubhalakṣaṇaḥ ||
content.translation: He is well-proportioned with symmetrically divided limbs, a smooth complexion, and great valor. He has a full chest, large eyes, is endowed with splendor, and bears auspicious marks.
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
धर्मज्ञः सत्यसन्धश्च प्रजानां च हिते रतः ।
यशस्वी ज्ञानसम्पन्नः शुचिर्वश्यः समाधिमान् ॥
content.transliteration:
dharmajñaḥ satyasandhaśca prajānāṃ ca hite rataḥ |
yaśasvī jñānasampannaḥ śucirvaśyaḥ samādhimān ||
content.translation: He knows Dharma, is true to his promises, and is devoted to the welfare of the people. He is illustrious, endowed with knowledge, pure, obedient to elders, and of a concentrated mind.
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
प्रजापतिसमः श्रीमान् धाता रिपुनिषूदनः ।
रक्षिता जीवलोकस्य धर्मस्य परिरक्षिता ॥
content.transliteration:
prajāpatisamaḥ śrīmān dhātā ripunisūdanaḥ |
rakṣitā jīvalokasya dharmasya parirakṣitā ||
content.translation: He is equal to Prajapati (the Creator), glorious, the sustainer, and the destroyer of enemies. He is the protector of all living beings and the supreme defender of Dharma.
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
रक्षिता स्वस्य धर्मस्य स्वजनस्य च रक्षिता ।
वेदवेदाङ्गतत्त्वज्ञो धनुर्वेदे च निष्ठितः ॥
content.transliteration:
rakṣitā svasya dharmasya svajanasya ca rakṣitā |
vedavedāṅgatattvajño dhanurvede ca niṣṭhitaḥ ||
content.translation: He is the protector of his own Dharma and the protector of his own people. He knows the true essence of the Vedas and Vedangas, and is highly proficient in the science of archery.
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.

content.sanskrit:
सर्वशास्त्रार्थतत्त्वज्ञः स्मृतिमान् प्रतिभानवान् ।
सर्वलोकप्रियः साधुरदीनात्मा विचक्षणः ॥
content.transliteration:
sarvaśāstrārthatattvajñaḥ smṛtimān pratibhānavān |
sarvalokapriyaḥ sādhuradīnātmā vicakṣaṇaḥ ||
content.translation: He knows the true meaning of all scriptures, possesses a great memory, and is brilliant. He is beloved by all the worlds, is gentle, un-depressed in spirit, and clear-sighted.
content.translation_source: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation (Project Gutenberg).
content.sanskrit_source: Ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification.
content.provenance: Meaning: Shoonaya literal study rendering, cross-checked against public-domain Griffith translation. Sanskrit: ancient Valmiki verse text; digital transcription source pending final rights verification. Numbering cross-checked against valmikiramayan.net.
